title: Zoro Stands Against Cabaji
description: On pages 5–8, Zoro faces Cabaji while Buggy’s crew tries to exploit his injuries and turn the duel into a spectacle.
scene: Zoro holds his ground against Cabaji’s tricks, treating the fight as a matter of pride rather than a simple brawl.

dialogue.speaker: Buggy
dialogue.text: gasp! It's the kid in the straw hat!! Cap'n buggy, watch out for him!!

dialogue.speaker: Buggy
dialogue.text: He's got special powers from eating the devil fruit-- just like you!!! He's a rubber man!!!

dialogue.speaker: Buggy
dialogue.text: THAT'S WHY MY BUGGY BALL BOUNCED OFF OF HIM!

dialogue.speaker: Buggy
dialogue.text: BUGGY'S SECOND MATE, CABAJ THE ACROBAT!!

dialogue.speaker: Buggy
dialogue.text: I'll cut 'em to Mince- Meat, Cap'n!!

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: IF IT'S A SWORD DUELL YOU WANT, I'M YOUR MAN!

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: RORO- NOA Zolo, as one swords- man to an- other

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: It will be an honor to cut you down.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: Hey, Zolo! Maybe you should rest.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: HE COULDN'T HAVE HEALED YET FROM THE WOUNDS THE CAPTAIN GAVE HIM. I'M SURPRISED HE CAN EVEN IS TAND...

summary: The part reinforces Zoro’s discipline and pride, showing that the Straw Hats will not let Buggy’s crew define the terms of the fight.
